description:
You are a deployment engineer rolling out a very small update.  
    A local bare repository already exists in /home/user/remote/sample-app.git.  
    Perform the following Git operations from the terminal (no GUI).  
    1. Clone the bare repo into /home/user/sample-app (create that exact directory via git clone).  
    2. Inside the new working copy, create and switch to a branch named release-2024-06-19.  
    3. Edit the file called VERSION that is already in the root of the project so that its full contents become exactly the single line:  
       2.0.1  
       (there must be a trailing newline).  
    4. Commit the change with the commit message:  
       Bump version to 2.0.1  
    5. Create the plain-text file /home/user/deployment/update.log (create any missing parent directory) containing **one line** with three tab-separated fields in the order shown below:  
         a) the full 40-character commit hash of the HEAD of branch release-2024-06-19  
         b) the literal branch name release-2024-06-19  
         c) the literal text Bump version to 2.0.1  
       Example of the required format (NOTE: the hash below is only an example, yours will differ):  
       0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef01234567	release-2024-06-19	Bump version to 2.0.1  
    Do **not** add extra spaces, quotes or additional lines—exactly one line with two tab characters.  
    6. You do not need to push anywhere.  
    After completing the work, the automated test will verify:  
       • /home/user/sample-app exists and is a Git repository.  
       • A branch named release-2024-06-19 exists and is checked out.  
       • VERSION contains exactly “2.0.1\n”.  
       • update.log exists at /home/user/deployment/ with the precise tab-delimited single-line format defined above, and that the commit hash recorded is the current HEAD of branch release-2024-06-19.  
    Produce no additional output files. No sudo rights are required.
truth:
<!-- Repository seed data (already present before the agent starts) -->
    The directory /home/user/remote/sample-app.git is an initialized bare repository.  
    It contains a single initial commit on branch main with one file named VERSION whose contents are exactly:
    2.0.0\n
    The initial commit has the message "Initial version 2.0.0".

    <!-- What the tests will check AFTER the agent finishes -->
    1. Directory /home/user/sample-app/.git exists (indicating a clone).  
    2. Inside that clone, `git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD` returns "release-2024-06-19".  
    3. File /home/user/sample-app/VERSION contains exactly the single line "2.0.1\n".  
    4. `git log -1 --pretty=%B` returns exactly "Bump version to 2.0.1".  
    5. The commit that HEAD points to is different from the initial commit (i.e. the repo contains at least two commits).  
    6. File /home/user/deployment/update.log exists and:  
       • consists of exactly one line.  
       • the line matches the regular expression:  
         ^[0-9a-f]{40}\trelease-2024-06-19\tBump version to 2.0.1\n$  
       • the first field (commit hash) equals `git rev-parse HEAD` inside /home/user/sample-app.  

    The automated grading script will clone and inspect /home/user/sample-app and read /home/user/deployment/update.log to perform the checks listed above.
name: task_000000_732f0477
